meta_description: Learn more about die casting products with Xometry, including how the die casting process works and what die casting can do for you. Xometry offers custom die casting quotes with zinc, magnesium, and aluminum alloys and a wide array of finishes.

sections.heading: Custom Die Casting Services
sections.text: Custom Die Casting Services Custom aluminum and zinc die casting for complex metal parts | Cost-effective tooling and rapid, scalable production | ISO 9001:2015, ISO 13485, IATF 16949:2016, AS9100D certified. ITAR registered. See How It Works

sections.heading: Die Casting Overview
sections.text: Die Casting Overview Die casting is a versatile, reliable manufacturing process ideal for producing high volumes of complex metal parts. Similar to plastic injection molding, it uses a custom steel toolâcalled a dieâto inject molten alloys such as aluminum and zinc under high pressure. This highly repeatable process produces near-net-shape components that reduce the need for secondary operations, though parts can be post-machined to meet tighter critical-to-function tolerances or receive a variety of surface finishes. Every die casting project requires careful attention to detail. At Xometry, our manufacturing experts and project managers manually review and quote every order to ensure...

sections.heading: Xometry's Die Casting Service
sections.text: Xometry's Die Casting Service Start A Quote Upload your files to the Xometry Quote Engine to configure and submit your project. Our team will consult with you to ensure your specifications, lead time, and pricing fit your project needs. A DFM report for approval is provided before tooling is produced. Sample Approval Once your project is kicked off, we will build a high-quality die. We will send out part samples, called T1s, for approval before proceeding with the production run. Production Begins Once you approve your samples, we move forward with production and deliver the parts. Xometry handles supplier management and logistics throughout the process. You own your tooling and can easily...

sections.heading: Built-In Tool Management
sections.text: Built-In Tool Management Xometry's platform has built-in features that make managing tooled projects a breeze, giving customers greater transparency into production. Find all your tooling quotes and orders in one space with your Tool Library . From there, you can explore their Tool Details page, which covers the current status, milestones, tool specifics, and activity related to that tool. You can also easily find and contact the Xometry team member assigned to your project, no matter the project's current phase. Our Tool Library and Tool Details pages provide multiple benefits, such as: Better Management of Tooled Quotes & Orders Tool Organization & Categorization Detailed Status Updates &...

sections.heading: Metal Materials for Die Cast Parts
sections.text: Metal Materials for Die Cast Parts Several different metal materials are available for die casting. The material you choose will depend on your intended use for the product. For instance, cast aluminum parts are common in aerospace and automotive applications where corrosion resistance and weight reduction are essential. You can learn more about the main types of die casting materials we offer in the sections below. If you have a specific material request, just let us know! Aluminum Die Casting Aluminum alloys are the most widely used for die-cast parts. Aluminum die casting suppliers benefit from the total recyclability of the material and its ease of use. Due to its higher melting point,...

sections.heading: Die Casting Services Available from Xometry
sections.text: Die Casting Services Available from Xometry Xometry partners with die casters that utilize the latest die casting machines and technology to deliver high-quality, on-demand parts to a wide variety of industries. Our offering focuses on the two leading types of die casting: hot chamber casting and cold chamber casting . Both types can produce parts with complex geometry, close tolerances, and robust mechanical properties. Hot Chamber Die Casting Also known as gooseneck casting, the hot chamber die casting process is the most popular die casting process. It is optimal for working with lower-melting-point alloys such as zinc, magnesium , and lead. A holding pot is filled with metal and heated...

sections.heading: Applications of Die Casting
sections.text: Applications of Die Casting The versatility and high production potential of die casting enable it for various applications and industries. Products that are small to medium in size with complex features are typically well-suited for the die casting process. Examples include consumer and industrial products, automotive parts , aerospace components, and medical device parts. Die casting products are a great option when you need high-volume metal parts that may be too large for similar high-volume processes such as Metal Injection Molding (MIM). Aerospace & Automotive Components Metal Housings Pumps and Plumbing Fixtures Outdoor lighting Gears Appliances and Lighting

sections.heading: Die Casting Advantages and Disadvantages
sections.text: Die Casting Advantages and Disadvantages Advantages of Die Casting Complex Geometry Die casting can reliably produce custom die cast parts with thin walls and challenging geometry that is durable and dimensionally stable. Strength Metal die castings are typically stronger than plastic injection molded parts and more resistant to high temperatures. Metals such as aluminum offer exceptional strength-to-weight ratios. Precision Die casting offers typical tolerances ranging from +/-0.003â³ â 0.005â³ per inch but can go as tight as +/- .001â depending on your specifications, part geometry, and material. Custom Finishes Metal finishers can produce cast parts with smooth or textured surfaces...

sections.heading: Why Choose Xometry for Online Die Casting Services?
sections.text: Why Choose Xometry for Online Die Casting Services? Endless Options Choose from millions of possible combinations of materials, finishes, tolerances, markings, and certifications for your order. Easy to Use Get started with our easy-to-use platform and let our experts take care of managing the project from locating the right manufacturing partner to delivery logistics. Vetted Network We are ISO 9001:2015, ISO 13485, and AS9100D certified. Only the top shops that apply to become Suppliers make it through our qualification process.
